title: MEDIUM: no CSRF token on OAuth authorize form
description: POST /oauth/authorize (src/api.rs line 553-573 and 595-690) is a standard HTML form submit with no CSRF protection. An attacker who knows (or guesses) the admin password can host a page that auto-submits the authorize form to grant an OAuth token to the attacker's app. The attacker needs: (1) the admin password, and (2) a registered client_id. Mitigation: embed a random CSRF token in a hidden field and validate it server-side on POST. This is the standard defense for login forms. Risk is partially mitigated by the password requirement, but CSRF protection is defense-in-depth.
close_reason: Wontfix: password-based auth on the form is CSRF-proof — attacker cannot pre-fill the password field
